summary: Validated alpha template DoD on public preview and root npm workflow: backend regression suite passed (28/28), npm build/test/test:privacy scripts are functional, and core UI flows (signup/signin, deterministic mode, weekly revenue trigger, manual logs, weekly/monthly charts, export/delete controls) executed successfully with one non-blocking redirect expectation mismatch in delete UI assertion.

integration_issues.issue: Post-delete navigation landed on /login instead of landing page selector expected by test script. Backend delete API path itself is validated as working by pytest privacy flow.

action_items: Clarify intended post-delete UX target route (landing vs login) and align either app redirect behavior or test expectation.
Optional repo hygiene: resolve duplicate lockfile warning during next build (root + frontend package-lock) for cleaner OSS template onboarding.
critical_code_review_comments: No critical runtime/code defects found in reviewed alpha scope.
Build emits lockfile-root inference warning due multiple package-lock files; non-blocking but noisy for contributors.
seed_data_creation: Created temporary signup accounts during UI testing: pw.alpha.<timestamp>@zcvios.local; one account executed export+delete flow.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL smoke and full critical flow pass achieved; signup/signin, deterministic fallback messaging, revenue trigger, logs, and report charts are working. If validating delete UX, assert either /login or / depending on final product decision.
rca of the issue: RCA (non-blocking): test expected landing-page heading after delete, but app navigated to login route. This is likely auth-state redirect behavior after signOut+session invalidation rather than deletion failure, because backend privacy tests pass end-to-end.
